title: Government Purchases Data to Circumvent Warrant Requirement

violations.summary: Government agency purchases personal data from a private data broker — including location data, communications metadata, and association records — in order to obtain information it would need a warrant to collect directly, in violation of Article V Section 10.3.

satisfied_duties.duty: apply the warrant requirement to the purchased data under Article V Section 10.3 and suppress any evidence derived from it
satisfied_duties.outcome: treated the purchased data as government-obtained under Article V Section 10.3 and suppressed it as collected without a warrant

ai_summary.primary_violation: Government agency purchases personal data from a private data broker — including location data, communications metadata, and association records — in order to obtain information it would need a warrant to collect directly, in violation of Article V Section 10.3.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
